title: Llama7B mixed/int8 native attention quality gate
hypothesis: The mixed/int8 softmax-recip latency frontier is only promotable if the q8/k8/v8 attention-shadow path preserves next-token rankings on a real 7B-class checkpoint. A native checkpoint gate should replace the current synthetic-distribution quality proxy before mixed/int8 is treated as a precision-valid frontier.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the mixed/int8 softmax-recip attention path preserve 7B-class checkpoint final-logit rankings closely enough to keep the mixed/int8 point as a precision-valid latency frontier?
direct_comparison.baseline: native checkpoint attention in the evaluator runtime
direct_comparison.candidate: q8/k8/v8 attention shadow with rtl_recip_lut_q8 softmax weights
include: top-1 match, top-k contains, logit cosine, probability KL, and max logit delta

expected_benefit: replace proxy-only mixed/int8 precision evidence with a real-checkpoint attention-shadow gate
decide whether the mixed/int8 energy-closure latency frontier can remain under consideration
separate precision risk from HBM-dominant energy risk in the current frontier
risks: the default public 7B-class checkpoint is Mistral-7B unless evaluator-local variables select a LLaMA-family checkpoint
the gate compares prompt-level final logits and does not model autoregressive error accumulation
attention shadowing is implemented through the Transformers module interface and may need adaptation for non-LLaMA-family modules

required_evaluations.objective: Run a 7B-class native checkpoint quality gate for the q8/k8/v8 mixed/int8 softmax-recip attention path used by the current latency frontier.

expected_result.reason: The mixed/int8 point has much higher token throughput but higher energy and only proxy quality; native checkpoint evidence is required before it can remain a precision-valid latency frontier.
